message:
Connectors: implement proper since_ts incremental filtering

All six connectors now properly respect the ScanContext.since_ts parameter
for watch-mode incremental indexing. Previously, some connectors would
process all messages and filter later, or not filter at all, causing
unnecessary work during watch reindexing.

Changes by connector:

**amp.rs**
- Use parse_timestamp() helper for flexible timestamp parsing (supports
  both i64 milliseconds and ISO-8601 strings)
- Apply since_ts filter during message extraction
- Re-assign message indices after filtering to maintain sequential order
- Remove redundant index re-assignment in outer loop

**claude_code.rs**
- Add since_ts filtering in both JSONL and JSON parsing branches using
  let-chain syntax for clean conditional skipping
- Extract sessionId and gitBranch metadata from first available entry
- Include session metadata in conversation.metadata for richer context

**cline.rs**
- Major restructure: prefer ui_messages.json (user-facing) over
  api_conversation_history.json to avoid duplicates
- Add since_ts filtering with let-chain syntax
- Support additional content field aliases (message)
- Skip empty content messages
- Sort messages by timestamp before indexing
- Extract workspace from metadata (rootPath/cwd/workspace keys)
- Derive started_at/ended_at from first/last message timestamps
- Generate fallback title from first message if metadata lacks one

**codex.rs**
- Add since_ts filtering in both modern JSONL and legacy JSON branches
- Uses let-chain syntax for clean conditional message skipping

**gemini.rs**
- Add since_ts filtering during message extraction
- Expand path extraction to support Windows paths:
  - Drive letters (C:\, D:/)
  - UNC paths (\\Server\Share)
  - Mixed separators
- Add comprehensive unit tests for path extraction across platforms
- Tighten minimum path length validation

**opencode.rs**
- Fix index assignment order: assign stable indices based on full history
  before filtering, not after (ensures consistent message ordering)
- Add db_path hash to external_id to disambiguate sessions across
  different OpenCode installations (prevents collision when same session
  ID exists in multiple .opencode directories)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
